column2:
The §MBetter Character Tooltips§! mod has a feature which allows §Pspacers§! to be shown at the top of the tooltip. The point of this feature is to allow the same piece of information to stay in the same exact spot on-screen when scrolling through the character finder. However, this requires a lot of vertical space, which may be undesirable - particularly at lower screen resolutions.

§PSpacers§! are §Ycurrently disabled§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.

By taking this decision, §Pspacers§! will be §Yenabled by default§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips, and each §Yplayer§! will gain access to an §Yindividual decision§! which allows them to disable spacers for their specific character.

The §MBetter Character Tooltips§! mod has a feature which allows §Pspacers§! to be shown at the top of the tooltip. The point of this feature is to allow the same piece of information to stay in the same exact spot on-screen when scrolling through the character finder. However, this requires a lot of vertical space, which may be undesirable - particularly at lower screen resolutions.

§PSpacers§! are §Ycurrently enabled by default§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.

By taking this decision, §Pspacers§! will be §Ydisabled§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.

The §MBetter Character Tooltips§! mod has a feature which allows §Pspacers§! to be shown at the top of the tooltip. The point of this feature is to allow the same piece of information to stay in the same exact spot on-screen when scrolling through the character finder. However, this requires a lot of vertical space, which may be undesirable - particularly at lower screen resolutions.

§PSpacers§! are §Ycurrently available for any players who want them§!, and are §Ycurrently disabled for your character specifically§!.

By taking this decision, §Pspacers§! will be §Yenabled for your character specifically§!.

The §MBetter Character Tooltips§! mod has a feature which allows §Pspacers§! to be shown at the top of the tooltip. The point of this feature is to allow the same piece of information to stay in the same exact spot on-screen when scrolling through the character finder. However, this requires a lot of vertical space, which may be undesirable - particularly at lower screen resolutions.

§PSpacers§! are §Ycurrently available for any players who want them§!, and are §Ycurrently enabled for your character specifically§!.

By taking this decision, §Pspacers§! will be §Ydisabled for your character specifically§!.

NOTE: This choice is NOT inherited: you will need to take this decision again after succession.

In case the §MBetter Character Tooltips§! mod's §Pspacers§! are not working properly, it is possible to enter §Pspacer debug mode§!, where the reason for each §Pspacer§! is shown on-screen.

INFO: The rule for showing a §Pspacer§! is that, if the §YBCT mod's logic§! "believes" that a character DOES NOT have a particular title (eg. "Prince of {X}"), then the relevant §Pspacer§! IS included in the tooltip (eg. "§g(NotPrince)§!").

Please notify the BCT mod developer if you discover any such bugs - especially if they are easily reproducible (eg. on starting a new game in with no other mods active)!

§PSpacer debugging§! is §Ycurrently disabled§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.

If you take this decision then §Pspacer debugging§! will be §Yenabled§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.

In case the §MBetter Character Tooltips§! mod's §Pspacers§! are not working properly, it is possible to enter §Pspacer debug mode§!, where the reason for each §Pspacer§! is shown on-screen.

INFO: The rule for showing a §Pspacer§! is that, if the §YBCT mod's logic§! "believes" that a character DOES NOT have a particular title (eg. "Prince of {X}"), then the relevant §Pspacer§! IS included in the tooltip (eg. "§g(NotPrince)§!").

Please notify the BCT mod developer if you discover any such bugs - especially if they are easily reproducible (eg. on starting a new game in with no other mods active)!

§PSpacer debugging§! is §Ycurrently enabled§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.

If you take this decision then §Pspacer debugging§! will be §Ydisabled§! in all §Yplayer characters'§! tooltips.
